title_en: Holding Firm to the Sunnah and the Community
title_id: Berpegang Teguh pada Sunnah dan Jama'ah
content_en:
Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal opens Usul al-Sunnah with what he regards as the most fundamental obligation upon every Muslim: holding firmly to the Sunnah of the Prophet, upon him be peace, and to the path of the Companions. Ahmad writes from the experience of a scholar who had personally endured the Mihna, the inquisition imposed by the Abbasid caliphate under the influence of Mu'tazilite theology, which demanded that scholars affirm the createdness of the Quran. His refusal to compromise under severe pressure, including imprisonment and flogging, gave his creedal positions an authority that went beyond mere scholarship. When Ahmad speaks of holding fast to the Sunnah, he speaks as one who had paid a personal price for that commitment.

Ahmad defines the Usul al-Sunnah as the positions transmitted from the Companions of the Prophet and from the early generations of Muslims. He emphasizes that the believer must follow what has been transmitted without adding to it or subtracting from it. The introduction of new formulations, even if they seem to defend orthodox positions, is treated with suspicion. This methodological conservatism reflects Ahmad's conviction that the Companions understood the religion of Allah better than any later generation, and that their unanimous practice and belief constitutes a binding authority second only to the explicit texts of the Quran and the authenticated Sunnah of the Prophet.

The opening section also establishes a clear warning against innovation (bid'ah). Ahmad regards every innovation in matters of religion as a deviation, citing the well-known hadith that every newly invented matter in religion is an error and every error leads to the Fire. He applies this warning with particular force to theological innovations: new ways of talking about Allah, new formulations of creedal positions, and new methods of interpreting the divine attributes that have no basis in the transmitted practice of the early Muslims. The Mu'tazilite and Jahmite schools, in his view, are precisely the kind of innovation that must be rejected, because they introduce rationalist frameworks that override the plain testimony of revelation.

Ahmad's insistence on the Jama'a, the community, is equally prominent. He identifies the true community as those who follow the Sunnah of the Prophet and the path of his Companions, not simply those who comprise the numerical majority or who hold political power. The believer who holds to the Sunnah is part of the true Jama'a even if they are few, while the one who follows innovation is outside it even if they are numerically dominant. This conception of the community as defined by adherence to transmitted knowledge rather than by institutional or political criteria was characteristic of the early hadith scholars and gave the movement represented by Ahmad its distinctive character as a force of religious continuity against the pressure of rationalist theology.
content_id:
Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal membuka Ushul al-Sunnah dengan apa yang ia anggap sebagai kewajiban paling fundamental bagi setiap Muslim: berpegang teguh pada Sunnah Nabi ﷺ dan pada jalan para sahabat. Ahmad menulis dari pengalaman seorang ulama yang secara pribadi telah menanggung Mihnah, inkuisisi yang diberlakukan oleh kekhalifahan Abbasiyah di bawah pengaruh teologi Mu'tazilah, yang mengharuskan para ulama menegaskan sifat makhluk dari Al-Quran. Penolakkannya untuk berkompromi di bawah tekanan berat, termasuk pemenjaraan dan cambukan, memberikan posisi-posisi aqidahnya otoritas yang melampaui sekadar keilmuan. Ketika Ahmad berbicara tentang berpegang teguh pada Sunnah, ia berbicara sebagai seseorang yang telah membayar harga pribadi untuk komitmen tersebut.

Ahmad mendefinisikan Ushul al-Sunnah sebagai posisi-posisi yang diriwayatkan dari para sahabat Nabi dan dari generasi-generasi awal kaum Muslim. Ia menekankan bahwa seorang mukmin harus mengikuti apa yang diriwayatkan tanpa menambahkan kepadanya atau menguranginya. Pengenalan formulasi-formulasi baru, bahkan jika tampaknya membela posisi-posisi ortodoks, diperlakukan dengan kecurigaan. Konservatisme metodologis ini mencerminkan keyakinan Ahmad bahwa para sahabat memahami agama Allah lebih baik daripada generasi mana pun yang lebih belakangan, dan bahwa praktik dan keyakinan mereka yang bulat merupakan otoritas yang mengikat setelah teks-teks eksplisit Al-Quran dan Sunnah Nabi yang terautentikasi.

Bagian pembuka ini juga menegakkan peringatan yang jelas terhadap bid'ah. Ahmad menganggap setiap bid'ah dalam masalah agama sebagai penyimpangan, mengutip hadis yang terkenal bahwa setiap perkara yang dibuat-buat dalam agama adalah kesesatan dan setiap kesesatan menuju Neraka. Ia menerapkan peringatan ini dengan kekuatan khusus pada inovasi-inovasi teologis: cara-cara baru berbicara tentang Allah, formulasi-formulasi baru dari posisi-posisi aqidah, dan metode-metode baru dalam menafsirkan sifat-sifat ilahi yang tidak memiliki dasar dalam praktik yang diriwayatkan dari kaum Muslim awal. Mazhab Mu'tazilah dan Jahmiyah, dalam pandangannya, adalah justru jenis inovasi yang harus ditolak, karena mereka memperkenalkan kerangka-kerangka rasionalis yang mengabaikan kesaksian wahyu yang jelas.

Penekanan Ahmad pada Jama'ah, komunitas, sama menonjolnya. Ia mengidentifikasi komunitas yang benar sebagai mereka yang mengikuti Sunnah Nabi dan jalan para sahabatnya, bukan sekadar mereka yang merupakan mayoritas secara numerik atau yang memegang kekuasaan politik. Orang beriman yang berpegang pada Sunnah adalah bagian dari Jama'ah yang benar meskipun mereka sedikit, sementara mereka yang mengikuti bid'ah berada di luar jama'ah meskipun mereka secara numerik dominan. Konsepsi komunitas yang didefinisikan oleh kepatuhan pada pengetahuan yang diriwayatkan daripada oleh kriteria institusional atau politik ini merupakan ciri khas ulama-ulama hadis awal dan memberikan gerakan yang diwakili oleh Ahmad karakternya yang khas sebagai kekuatan kesinambungan agama melawan tekanan teologi rasionalis.
content_ar: الإمام أحمد ابن حنبل opens الأصول al-السنة with what he regards as the most الأساسي الالتزام upon every مسلم: holding firmly to the السنة of النبي ﷺ, upon him be peace, and to the الطريق of the الصحابة. أحمد writes from the experience of a scholar who had personally endured the Mihna, the inquisition imposed by the Abbasid الخلافة under the influence of Mu'tazilite theology, which demanded that العلماء affirm the createdness of the القرآن الكريم. His refusal to compromise under severe pressure, بما في ذلك imprisonment and flogging, gave his creedal positions an authority that went beyond mere scholarship. عندما أحمد speaks of holding fast to the السنة, he speaks as one who had paid a personal price for that commitment. أحمد defines the الأصول al-السنة as the positions transmitted from the الصحابة of النبي ﷺ and from the early generations of المسلمون. He emphasizes that the المؤمن must follow what has been transmitted without adding to it or subtracting from it. The introduction of new formulations, even إذا they seem to defend orthodox positions, is treated with suspicion. This methodological conservatism reflects أحمد's conviction that the الصحابة understood the religion of الله better than any later generation, and that their unanimous practice and الاعتقاد constitutes a binding authority ثانياً only to the explicit texts of the القرآن الكريم and the authenticated السنة of النبي ﷺ. The opening الفصل أيضاً establishes a clear warning against البدعة (البدعة). أحمد regards every البدعة in matters of religion as a deviation, citing the well-known الحديث that every newly invented matter in religion is an error and every error leads to the Fire. He applies this warning with particular force to theological innovations: new ways of talking about الله, new formulations of creedal positions, and new methods of interpreting the divine attributes that have no basis in the transmitted practice of the early المسلمون. The Mu'tazilite and Jahmite schools, in his view, are precisely the kind of البدعة that must be rejected, لأن they introduce rationalist frameworks that override the plain testimony of الوحي. أحمد's insistence on the Jama'a, the الجماعة, is equally prominent. He identifies the true الجماعة as those who follow the السنة of النبي ﷺ and the الطريق of his الصحابة, not simply those who comprise the numerical majority or who hold political power. The المؤمن who holds to the السنة is part of the true Jama'a even إذا they are few, بينما the one who follows البدعة is outside it even إذا they are numerically dominant. This conception of the الجماعة as defined by adherence to transmitted العلم rather than by institutional or political criteria was characteristic of the early الحديث العلماء and gave the movement represented by أحمد its distinctive الأخلاق as a force of religious continuity against the pressure of rationalist theology.
